result.markdown:
--- title: "Build a design matrix" subtitle: "Model event timing with a typed spec and inspect the regressors." jupyter: python3 --- ## Create Events ::: {#64d3b728 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.spec import hrf, compile as compile_spec from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": [0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40], "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"], "response_time": [0.8, 1.1, 0.7, 0.9, 1.0, 1.2], "duration": 2.0, }) events ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
onset condition response_time duration
0 0 face 0.8 2.0
1 8 scene 1.1 2.0
2 16 face 0.7 2.0
3 24 scene 0.9 2.0
4 32 face 1.0 2.0
5 40 scene 1.2 2.0
``` ::: ::: ## Define the Acquisition Grid ::: {#863641c3 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=60, TR=1.0) sf.samples[:8] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` array([0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5]) ``` ::: ::: ## Build the Design from a Typed Spec `hrf(...)` is a typed value. `compile()` lowers a spec against the events and sampling frame into an `EventModel` (and its baseline) — no fitting, no string formula. The design matrix and column metadata are inspectable immediately. ::: {#ee918d22 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} spec = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1") event_model, baseline_model = compile_spec(spec, data=events, sampling_frame=sf) event_model.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` (60, 2) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#95b6d240 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} event_model.column_names ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` ['condition_condition.face', 'condition_condition.scene'] ``` ::: ::: ## Inspect Column Metadata `design_colmap` reports the provenance of every column — which condition, which basis, what role — for the compiled event model. ::: {#ef831964 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} design_colmap(event_model)[["col", "name", "condition", "role", "basis_name"]] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
col name condition role basis_name
0 1 condition_condition.face condition.face task SPMG1
1 2 condition_condition.scene condition.scene task SPMG1
``` ::: ::: ## Add a Parametric Modulator Terms compose with `+`. A second `hrf(...)` on a continuous column adds a parametric regressor — still a typed spec, still inspected before fitting. ::: {#2d91c7bf .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} modulated = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1") + hrf("response_time", basis="spmg1") mod_model, _ = compile_spec(modulated, data=events, sampling_frame=sf) mod_model.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` (60, 3) ``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Linear model](linear-model.qmd) - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) - [Overview and workflow](overview-workflow.qmd)
